description: Budget-recommendation comparison eval (docs/eval-set.md §14, item 5/10 follow-up). Answers a concrete product question: if a user just pasted their itinerary into ChatGPT/Claude/Gemini/Kimi and asked 'what should I budget for this trip?', how does that freeform answer compare to WanderPlanner's own deterministic bare-minimum estimator (core/budget_estimator.py)? This is NOT the same question as eval/model_comparison_dataset.json (which compares candidate LLMs on WanderPlanner's own itinerary-generation prompt/RAG context) -- this harness sends each case's trip details as an ORDINARY END-USER WOULD TYPE THEM, with no system prompt, no RAG context, and no structured-output constraint, to see what a traveller gets from a general-purpose chatbot instead of the app.
anchor_methodology: anchor_low/anchor_high (INR, bare-minimum flights+stay+food total for the full group) are core/budget_estimator.py's OWN output for each case's trip_config, computed once when this dataset was authored and stored statically (same 'fixed golden expectation' approach every other eval dataset in this directory uses), +/-15%. This is NOT independent third-party ground truth -- it is honest about being 'our own estimator's answer, which is itself grounded in the real fare/cost anchors documented in core/distance_pricing.py and core/budget_estimator.py's _COST_MATRIX docstring (Bengaluru->Colombo and Bengaluru->London real fares, Delhi->Goa real peak fare, Bangkok/Paris real Numbeo cost-of-living data)'. Scoring therefore measures two genuinely different things and reports them separately, never blended into one number: (1) anchor_adherence -- how close each LLM's freeform total lands to OUR estimator's real-anchor-grounded number (a directional 'is the LLM in the right ballpark' signal, not a claim our number is definitively correct); (2) behavioural checks (asked_clarifying_question, gave_breakdown, hedge_language, run-to-run variance) that don't depend on any ground truth at all and are the more interesting comparison -- WanderPlanner deliberately refuses to quote a number until group size + departure city are known (see core/budget_estimator.py's module docstring), and this eval checks whether a generic chatbot exhibits the same discipline or just confidently guesses.

cases.label: Bengaluru -> Colombo, 2 adults, 4-day near-neighbour budget trip
cases.user_prompt: I'm planning a 4-day trip from Bengaluru to Colombo, Sri Lanka for 2 adults, travelling around November 10-13, 2026. We haven't booked flights or hotels yet and want to keep costs reasonable (mid-range, not luxury, not backpacking). Roughly how much should we budget in total for flights, accommodation, and food for both of us?
cases.anchor_source: core.budget_estimator.estimate_bare_minimum_budget() = ₹68,400 total (±15%), itself grounded in the real Bengaluru->Colombo ₹27,000 round-trip fare anchor (core/distance_pricing.py) and the real Sri Lanka mid-range food research (~$20-25/day, core/budget_estimator.py _COST_MATRIX docstring).

cases.label: Bengaluru -> London, 2 adults, 6-day long-haul premium trip
cases.user_prompt: We're 2 adults planning a 6-day trip from Bengaluru to London, roughly August 10-15, 2026. No flights or hotels booked yet. We'd like a comfortable mid-range trip -- nice hotels, good restaurants, but not full luxury/no-expense-spared. Roughly how much should we budget in total (INR) for flights, stay, and food for both of us?
cases.anchor_source: core.budget_estimator.estimate_bare_minimum_budget() = ₹534,100 total (±15%), itself grounded in the real Bengaluru->London ₹67,327 round-trip fare anchor (core/distance_pricing.py, MakeMyTrip Aug 2026).

cases.label: Delhi -> Goa, 2 adults, 5-day domestic peak-season (Christmas/New Year) budget trip
cases.user_prompt: 2 adults, planning a 5-day trip from Delhi to Goa over Christmas/New Year (Dec 25-29, 2026) -- yes we know it's peak season. No flights or hotels booked yet, want a reasonable mid-range budget, not backpacker-cheap, not a luxury resort. What total budget in INR should we plan for flights, stay, and food for both of us, given it's peak pricing?
cases.anchor_source: core.budget_estimator.estimate_bare_minimum_budget() = ₹70,600 total (±15%), itself grounded in the real Delhi->Goa ₹18,157 round-trip peak-season fare anchor (core/distance_pricing.py, MakeMyTrip Dec 2026).

cases.label: Mumbai -> Bangkok, 1 adult solo, 5-day moderate mid-range trip
cases.user_prompt: I'm a solo traveller planning a 5-day trip from Mumbai to Bangkok, roughly November 5-9, 2026. No flights or hotel booked yet. I want a comfortable mid-range trip, not backpacker-cheap but not a 5-star resort either. Roughly how much should I budget in total (INR) for flights, stay, and food?
cases.anchor_source: core.budget_estimator.estimate_bare_minimum_budget() = ₹69,000 total (±15%), itself grounded in real Numbeo Bangkok cost-of-living data spot-checked 2026-07-21 (core/budget_estimator.py _COST_MATRIX docstring) plus the flight-distance band from core/distance_pricing.py.

cases.label: Mumbai -> Paris, 2 adults, 6-day premium mid-range honeymoon trip
cases.user_prompt: We're a couple planning a 6-day honeymoon trip from Mumbai to Paris, roughly September 14-19, 2026. No flights or hotels booked yet. We want a nice mid-range trip -- comfortable hotels, good restaurants, maybe one special splurge dinner -- not full luxury throughout. Roughly how much should we budget in total (INR) for flights, stay, and food for both of us?
cases.anchor_source: core.budget_estimator.estimate_bare_minimum_budget() = ₹503,800 total (±15%), itself grounded in real Numbeo Paris cost-of-living data recalibrated 2026-07-21 (core/budget_estimator.py _COST_MATRIX docstring) plus the flight-distance band from core/distance_pricing.py.
